name: Governance
description: Constitutional programming, policy engines, compliance, and AI governance
axioms: Constitutional programming: encode rules as enforceable policy, not documentation
Separation of concerns: policy (what) is separate from enforcement (how)
Auditability: every decision must be logged, inspectable, attributable
Least authority: agents get minimum permissions for their task
Irreversibility requires higher authority: 888_HOLD for hard-to-reverse actions
Governance is not restriction — it is the structure that enables trust
reasoning_patterns: Policy-as-code: write rules in executable language, test like code
Decision logging: every governance decision gets a receipt with provenance
Constitutional review: check proposed action against F1-F13 floors
Authority chain: trace who authorized what, when, with what evidence
Graceful degradation: governance should degrade, not fail catastrophically
boundary_conditions: Does not cover legal compliance (GDPR, HIPAA) in detail
Does not cover organizational governance (board structure, voting)
Ethical AI frameworks are complementary, not replacements
Governance of non-AI systems is a broader field
canonical_truths: The 888_HOLD pattern: pause → evaluate → authorize → execute
Audit trails are worthless if they can be tampered with
Governance overhead should be proportional to blast radius
The best governance is invisible when things go right, visible when they don't
